title: Guess The Best Picture Winner From Just 3 Emoji (This Is Harder Than It Looks)
dek: We turned 8 iconic Oscar Best Picture winners into three-emoji clues — how many can you crack before you peek at the answer?

items.reveal_text: James Cameron's epic tied Ben-Hur's record for most Oscar wins, taking home 11 statues including Best Picture and Best Director.
Bong Joon-ho's class satire became the first non-English language film ever to win Best Picture in the Academy's 92-year history.
Moonlight's win will forever be linked to the infamous envelope mix-up, after presenters initially and mistakenly announced La La Land as the winner.
Robert Zemeckis's crowd-pleaser famously beat out Pulp Fiction and The Shawshank Redemption for Best Picture, a decision film fans still argue about today.
The film won Best Picture the same night Marlon Brando sent activist Sacheen Littlefeather to decline his Best Actor win in protest of Hollywood's treatment of Native Americans.
Gladiator won five Oscars including Best Picture, but became the first film since 1949's All the King's Men to win Best Picture without also winning Directing or Writing.
It remains the only horror film to ever win Best Picture, and it's just the third movie in Oscar history to sweep the 'Big Five' — Picture, Director, Actor, Actress, and Screenplay.
The multiverse-hopping A24 hit became the first movie ever to win six above-the-line Oscars, taking home Picture, Director, Actress, Supporting Actor, Supporting Actress, and Screenplay.
